items.title: Middle East war de-escalation rhetoric drove a sharp reversal in crude and a risk-on bounce
items.summary: - Event: Middle East war de-escalation rhetoric drove a sharp reversal in crude and a risk-on bounce.

items.title: Macro de-escalation signal around the Iran war is easing immediate oil/inflation stress; broad risk sentiment improved. No validated single-name thesis changer found across David's watchlist in this scan
items.summary: Macro de-escalation signal around the Iran war is easing immediate oil/inflation stress; broad risk sentiment improved. No validated single-name thesis changer found across David's watchlist in this scan

items.time: 2026-03-02 10:38 HKT — Material macro shock: Hormuz disruption after U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran
items.title: Multiple major outlets (Reuters, Bloomberg, NYT, Guardian, CNBC live coverage) report that after U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran, Tehran signaled/attempted closure pressure in the Strait of Hormuz and shipping flow was disrupted, with multiple tanker owners/oil majors/traders suspending transit.
items.summary: Multiple major outlets (Reuters, Bloomberg, NYT, Guardian, CNBC live coverage) report that after U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran, Tehran signaled/attempted closure pressure in the Strait of Hormuz and shipping flow was disrupted, with multiple tanker owners/oil majors/traders suspending transit.

items.time: 2026-03-02 10:38 HKT — Semiconductor demand datapoint (supportive, not a fresh shock)
items.title: Reuters + cross-pickups report South Korea February exports beat forecasts; semiconductor exports rose ~160.9% YoY and remained above $20B for a third consecutive month.
items.summary: Reuters + cross-pickups report South Korea February exports beat forecasts; semiconductor exports rose ~160.9% YoY and remained above $20B for a third consecutive month.

items.time: 2026-02-26 05:57 HKT — Material break: NVIDIA Q4 beat + above-consensus Q1 guide
items.title: NVIDIA official results showed Q4 FY2026 revenue of $68.1B and non-GAAP EPS of $1.62, with Q1 revenue guidance of $78B ±2%, which cleared most pre-release consensus ranges cited by major outlets.
items.summary: NVIDIA official results showed Q4 FY2026 revenue of $68.1B and non-GAAP EPS of $1.62, with Q1 revenue guidance of $78B ±2%, which cleared most pre-release consensus ranges cited by major outlets.

items.time: 2026-02-25 21:57 HKT — USTR says U.S. tariff rate will rise to 15% or higher for some countries
items.title: Reuters reported U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said tariff rates for some countries will be raised from the new 10% baseline to 15% or higher.
items.summary: Reuters reported U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said tariff rates for some countries will be raised from the new 10% baseline to 15% or higher.

items.time: 2026-02-25 12:57 HKT — Provisional: U.S. official says no Nvidia H200 chips have been sold to Chinese customers yet
items.title: Reuters (single-source official comment) reported a U.S. Commerce Department official said Nvidia's H200 AI chips have not yet been sold to Chinese customers.
items.summary: Reuters (single-source official comment) reported a U.S. Commerce Department official said Nvidia's H200 AI chips have not yet been sold to Chinese customers.

items.time: 2026-02-25 11:28 HKT — White House says U.S. working to raise temporary tariff rate from 10% to 15%
items.title: Reuters reported a White House official said the administration is working to lift the temporary global tariff rate to 15% from 10%.
items.summary: Reuters reported a White House official said the administration is working to lift the temporary global tariff rate to 15% from 10%.

items.time: 2026-02-25 11:27 HKT — U.S. judge lets anti-American hiring-bias lawsuit against Tesla proceed
items.title: Reuters reported a U.S. judge refused to dismiss a lawsuit alleging Tesla discriminated against American citizens in hiring, allowing the case to continue.
items.summary: Reuters reported a U.S. judge refused to dismiss a lawsuit alleging Tesla discriminated against American citizens in hiring, allowing the case to continue.

items.time: 2026-02-24 06:29 HKT — Tesla files suit against California DMV over false-advertising ruling
items.title: Tesla sued the California DMV seeking to reverse/contest a ruling tied to allegedly misleading marketing around Autopilot/FSD terminology.
items.summary: Tesla sued the California DMV seeking to reverse/contest a ruling tied to allegedly misleading marketing around Autopilot/FSD terminology.

items.time: 2026-02-24 06:28 HKT — IBM plunges ~13% on AI-disruption narrative (Anthropic COBOL tooling)
items.title: IBM shares fell about 13% in U.S. trading after Anthropic highlighted new Claude Code capabilities around COBOL analysis/modernization, which investors interpreted as a threat to a legacy modernization profit pool tied to IBM.
items.summary: IBM shares fell about 13% in U.S. trading after Anthropic highlighted new Claude Code capabilities around COBOL analysis/modernization, which investors interpreted as a threat to a legacy modernization profit pool tied to IBM.

items.time: 2026-02-24 01:28 HKT — Update: tariff shock broadens into deeper risk-off move
items.title: Follow-through reports (Reuters market coverage) show the earlier tariff-policy whipsaw has translated into a broader equity drawdown during U.S. cash trading, with tech-heavy indices and mega-cap growth under renewed pressure as investors reprice trade-policy uncertainty.
items.summary: Follow-through reports (Reuters market coverage) show the earlier tariff-policy whipsaw has translated into a broader equity drawdown during U.S. cash trading, with tech-heavy indices and mega-cap growth under renewed pressure as investors reprice trade-policy uncertainty.

items.time: 2026-02-24 00:28 HKT — Macro trade-policy shock (U.S. tariffs legal whipsaw)
items.title: Multiple Reuters updates indicate a major policy whipsaw: after a U.S. Supreme Court ruling struck down a broad set of prior tariffs, President Trump signaled/announced a temporary across-the-board tariff increase (10% to 15%) and further tariff threats tied to trade-deal compliance. Markets reportedly sold off on renewed policy uncertainty.
items.summary: Multiple Reuters updates indicate a major policy whipsaw: after a U.S. Supreme Court ruling struck down a broad set of prior tariffs, President Trump signaled/announced a temporary across-the-board tariff increase (10% to 15%) and further tariff threats tied to trade-deal compliance. Markets reportedly sold off on renewed policy uncertainty.
